title: LIVE: MoveProjectModal/Export/Import workers crash with AttributeError (self.call_from_thread on ModalScreen)
evidence: app.py used self.call_from_thread inside ModalScreen; call_from_thread exists on App not Screen; move succeeded then process exited non-zero (user-reported traceback)
impact: TUI crashes after a successful move/export/import; app exits non-zero; user sees a scary traceback despite success
recommended_action: Change self.call_from_thread -> self.app.call_from_thread in the three modals
public_behavior_change: No (fixes crash; behavior now completes cleanly)
validation: pytest 56 passed; manual reasoning of textual API

title: Zip-Slip: cli_restore extractall without member path sanitization
evidence: ocman.py zipf.extractall(restore_dir) at 6786 and 6923 with no per-member path check; a crafted backup ZIP with ../ or absolute members can write outside temp dir during extraction
impact: A malicious/corrupt backup ZIP can write files to arbitrary paths (local privilege/overwrite risk)
recommended_action: Add safe-extract helper validating each member resolves under the destination root; reject traversal/absolute

title: Connection leak on error path: 2nd sqlite3.connect in bundle_session_data not in try/finally
evidence: ocman.py ~5456 opens a second connection inside 'with zipfile' and closes only on success path (~5491); exception between leaves it open
impact: Leaked SQLite connection/handle on export failure; repeated failures accumulate handles/locks
recommended_action: Wrap the second connection in try/finally (or use contextlib.closing)

title: Unbounded full-file reads of potentially large exports/context into memory
evidence: load_export_file path.read_text() (1662) and load_prior_context_files (3138) read whole files; docstring notes exports can be tens of MB
impact: High memory footprint on very large sessions; possible OOM on constrained hosts
recommended_action: Document limitation now; streaming refactor is Medium-High risk (functionality) so defer the refactor

title: Possible None-subscript / unbound locals in CompactionScreen (LSP-flagged)
evidence: app.py:1319 object-of-None subscript; 1422-1424 possibly-unbound session_title/time_created_str/time_updated_str per type checker
impact: Potential AttributeError/UnboundLocalError on unusual compaction paths
recommended_action: Inspect and guard the flagged lines; add fallbacks

title: CHANGELOG.md missing [1.0.3] entry
evidence: CHANGELOG top entry is [1.0.2]; pyproject/version is 1.0.3
impact: Users cannot see what changed in 1.0.3; release hygiene
recommended_action: Add a [1.0.3] CHANGELOG entry summarizing move/export/import progress + Windows/streaming changes

title: Two hardcoded __version__ strings can drift
evidence: ocman.py:191 and ocman_tui/__init__.py:5 both hardcode 1.0.3 independently
impact: Version drift between CLI and TUI on future bumps
recommended_action: Make ocman_tui import __version__ from ocman (single source) if safe; else document the sync requirement

title: README clone URL vs actual remote drift
evidence: README: git clone .../fariello/ocman.git; remote is fariello/opencode-recover.git
impact: Users may clone a nonexistent/renamed repo
recommended_action: Confirm canonical URL with user; align README
